LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS/CROSS DEFENDANTS BRANDON KAPLAND, SUSAN CLARK, AND, KENNETH KAPLAN MOTION TO DISQUALIFY DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. HALL'S REPRESENTATION OF PLAINTIFFS ENDED IN JUNE 2008. AFTER ONE CONSULTATION, THAT DID NOT CONCERN THE 3443-20TH (MISSION) STREET PROPERTY THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS LITIGATION, CLARK FAILED TO FOLLOW THROUGH WITH THE PERMIT HISTORY AND PLAINTIFFS INITIATED NO FURTHER CONTACT WITH HALL WHO CLOSED HIS FILE JUNE 17, 2008. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW THE NATURE OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION HALL OBTAINED DURING THE ONE CONSULTATION MEETING AND HOW IT RELATES TO THE CURRENT LITIGATION. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW HALL PROVIDED ADVICE ON A LEGAL ISSUE CLOSELY RELATED TO AN ISSUE RAISED IN THIS CASE. THE MISSION STREET PROPERTY WAS NOT DISCUSSED DURING THE MEETING. THE ADVERSE IMPACT ON DEFENDANTS IF HALL AND ASSOCIATES COULD NOT REPRESENT THEM WOULD BE SUBSTANTIAL. GUTIERREZ HAS PREVIOUSLY SUCCESSFULLY REPRESENTED THEM IN AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER INVOLVING THE SAME APARTMENT. THE COURT ADDS: OBJECTION TO GUTIERREZ DECL. IS SUSTAINED; AND CLARK DECLARATION IS STRICKEN. (ORDER ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANTS' EVIDENCE FILED IN OPEN COURT.) ORDER RE: TODAY'S RULING SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Deft'S Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-15-2009 Advanced From Feb-02-10 To Dec-31-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Deft'S Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-31-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS/CROSS DEFENDANTS BRANDON KAPLAND, SUSAN CLARK, and, KENNETH KAPLAN Motion To Disqualify Defendants' Counsel. Appearances: Andrew M. Zacks, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Francisco A. Gutierrez, Esq. & Michael Hall, Esq. of M C Hall & Associates appearing for Defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Hall's representation of plaintiffs ended in June 2008. After one consultation, that did not concern the 3443-20th (Mission) Street property that is the subject of this litigation, Clark failed to follow through with the permit history and plaintiffs initiated no further contact with Hall who closed his file June 17, 2008. Plaintiffs fail to show the nature of any confidential information Hall obtained during the one consultation meeting and how it relates to the current litigation. Plaintiffs fail to show Hall provided advice on a legal issue closely related to an issue raised in this case. The Mission Street property was not discussed during the meeting. The adverse impact on defendants if Hall and Associates could not represent them would be substantial. Gutierrez has previously successfully represented them in an unlawful detainer involving the same apartment. The Court adds: Objection to Gutierrez Decl. is sustained; and Clark Declaration is stricken. (Order on Plaintiff's Objections to Defendants' evidence filed in Open Court.) Order re: today's ruling signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Nov-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS BRANDON KAPLAND, SUSAN CLARK, and, KENNETH KAPLAN Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings to Defendant's First Amended Answer. Appearances: James Brandan Kraus, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100 appearing for Cross-defendants; Francisco Gutierrez, Esq. Law Office of Michael Hall, (415) 512-9865 defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for judgment on the pleadings with respect to defendants' answer is denied. The legal and factual sufficiency of the affirmative defenses should be addressed on a summary motion, not a non-evidentiary motion such as this. Order signed in open Court. Order signed in open Court.(302/HEK)JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR # 7235
